title: Flag value completion
goal: Extend the bash/zsh completion scripts to complete a flag's enumerated values when the previous word is such a flag (e.g. 'mini done --bump <Tab>' offers none/patch/minor/major). Make commander the source of truth by giving --bump an explicit .choices([...]) and deriving the value sets from option.argChoices, so they never drift. Verifiable via snapshot tests.

steps.title: --bump uses commander .choices()
steps.detail: Replace the custom parseBumpLevel validator on the done and auto --bump options with .choices(['none','patch','minor','major']) so commander validates membership and exposes option.argChoices; keep the typed union value (cast as needed) and remove parseBumpLevel if it becomes unused.

steps.title: Renderer completes flag values
steps.detail: Extend the flag shape to carry optional value choices ({ name, values? }); both renderers gain a value branch keyed on '<cmd>:<prevWord>' that completes the choices when the previous word is a flag with a known value set — placed before the flag-name / file-fallback branches. Renderers stay pure/snapshot-friendly.

steps.title: CLI derives choices; tests; docs
steps.detail: In cli.ts read each option's argChoices into the flag's values; update render.test.ts and completion.test.ts to the new shape with value-completion cases + refreshed snapshots; mention flag-value completion in docs/non-interactive/completion.md and the README row; run typecheck and the full test suite.
